_doc: D82 — Sample-side tag manifest. SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for each test sample's canonical GoF tag(s). Consumed by Codebase/Frontend/playwright/tests/all-samples.spec.ts to resolve co-emit ambiguity DETERMINISTICALLY: the e2e clicks the candidate chip whose label matches a sample's known tag (in priority order) instead of arbitrarily picking the first chip. Co-emit ambiguity itself stays by design per DESIGN_DECISIONS.md D21/D38/D80 — this manifest only feeds the SAMPLE's known answer back into the TEST. 'tags' are the exact canonical labels chips/badges render (Builder, Singleton, MethodChaining, Adapter, Decorator, Proxy, Pimpl, Strategy, Factory, ...). kind=negative rows carry tags:[] by design. Add a sample -> add a row here, or the spec's drift guard fails fast.

samples.note: D63 algorithm-known-limit: matcher co-emits Builder for fluent `return *this` chains lacking a build() terminator. Either chip satisfies the contract; MethodChaining preferred.

D81 co-emit: the wrapper implements a shared interface and forwards to a same-interface member, so Adapter + Decorator co-emit (Proxy intentionally absent). Any wrapping-family chip satisfies the contract.
D81 per-class isolation: only Logger (Singleton) tags; the sibling Calculator is a plain class by design.
